mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals acute labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2% annualized during expansionary phases signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth >2% during expansions fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions exceeding 1% annualized signal severe demand destruction that forces immediate equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts >1%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate creates a negative real yield environment that compresses equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth alongside flat or falling FEDFUNDS yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 per month during recovery phases confirms labor market normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth >150k during recovery phases produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 2% signals acute supply chain disruption that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO falls >2%.
